query: Collagen peptide protein powder for endurance runners

parsed.idea: Collagen peptide protein powder for endurance runners
parsed.executive_summary: Collagen peptide protein powder targets a high-intent endurance running audience with a clinically supported joint and connective tissue recovery angle that differentiates from standard whey/plant protein incumbents. The market gap is real but crowded at the commodity level, requiring strong brand positioning and athlete partnerships to break through.

notes.demand: Collagen + running searches growing YoY; marathon/ultra community actively discusses joint health.
notes.competition: Vital Proteins, Ancient Nutrition, and Momentous already occupy this shelf with strong DTC presence.
notes.virality: Transformation content is harder for joint health vs aesthetics; niche run-community UGC is possible but limited ceiling.
notes.subscription: Monthly replenishment powder with 30-day supply is a natural subscription mechanic with high LTC.
notes.manufacturing: Collagen peptides are commodity inputs; flavoring and mixability are the only real formulation challenges.
notes.defensibility: Formula is easily replicated; defensibility must come from elite runner endorsements and community loyalty.

parsed.build_explanation: The endurance runner collagen niche has genuine demand and subscription economics but faces entrenched competitors with established brand trust. Validate with a direct-to-community DTC test targeting ultramarathon and trail running audiences before scaling ad spend.
parsed.path_to_10m: Partner with 3–5 elite ultramarathon athletes for authentic UGC, launch on Shopify with a subscription-first model targeting running club communities, and cross-sell into triathlon and cycling adjacencies to hit $10M ARR within 36 months.
market_gaps: No dominant brand owns the 'connective tissue recovery' narrative specifically for endurance runners vs strength athletes.
Most collagen products ignore Vitamin C co-dosing despite RCT evidence supporting it for collagen synthesis.
Unflavored single-ingredient SKUs rarely come with run-specific dosing protocols tied to training periodization.
Trail and ultra running communities are underserved by mainstream supplement brands who focus on gym culture.
No brand has built a strong community layer (training plans, coach partnerships) around a collagen supplement for runners.
brand_opportunities: Position as the official recovery supplement of the ultra and trail running community with race sponsorships.
Build a 'Run Long' content platform around injury prevention that drives organic SEO and brand authority.
Create a Vitamin C + Collagen dual-sachet system backed by the Shaw et al. RCT to signal clinical credibility.
Partner with physical therapists and running coaches as affiliate educators rather than just athlete influencers.
Develop a subscription bundle with electrolytes or magnesium to increase LTV and reduce single-SKU churn.

frustrations: My knees and tendons break down every time I ramp up mileage and I can never stay healthy long enough to PR.
Every protein powder is marketed to bodybuilders — nothing is designed for how runners actually train and recover.
desires: I want to run more miles without my joints falling apart so I can finally qualify for Boston.
I want a clean, science-backed supplement that fits into my morning routine before a long run.
fears: I'm afraid a chronic injury will end my running before I hit my big race goals.
I don't want to waste money on another supplement with no real evidence behind it.
ad_phrases: Built for the miles that break everyone else.
More miles. Less breakdown. Science-backed collagen for serious runners.

formula.notes: Shaw et al. 2017 RCT showed 15g before exercise increased collagen synthesis markers in tendons.
Essential co-factor for collagen biosynthesis; co-dosing is supported by the same RCT framework.
Rate-limiting amino acid in collagen synthesis; additional dosing may amplify connective tissue support.
Multiple RCTs show reduced muscle soreness and inflammation in endurance runners post-race.
Emerging evidence for joint lubrication and cartilage support relevant to high-mileage runners.

avoid.reason: Conflicts with the recovery and joint-health positioning; alienates runners who take this post-run or at night.
Clean-label is a core expectation in the trail and ultra running community; synthetic sweeteners trigger churn and negative UGC.

product_recommendation.dosing: One 20g scoop mixed in water or juice 30–60 minutes before a run or strength session; can double-dose on long run days above 15 miles.
product_recommendation.sub_ltv: $420/year based on 1 bag/month at $35/bag on 10% subscriber discount.
product_recommendation.notes: Single-serve stick packs for race-day and travel use should be a priority SKU for trial and retail placement.

regulatory_flags.notes: Avoid any claims around 'treating' tendinopathy or osteoarthritis; stick to structure/function language like 'supports joint health'.
